I was wondering why this was in here
The cover that comes on every Hetbahn bowls
are the same material used for the baby bottles (Polypropylene [PP])
I worried that a lot of environmental hormones/chemicals might be created from microwaving these bowls
But this material is perfectly fine to microwave or boil
So there's no need to worry

I really think that Hetbahn's instant rice is very delicious
It's best when you put it in the leftover ramyun noodle soup
That's 5
I forgot because I was too busy eating
There's 1 more I need to show you guys
This is UV sterilizer
These are made in an "aseptic clean room"
So these can be stored at room temperature for 9 months without any preservatives
Germs cause food to rot or expire
But they make sure that no germs get in here
That's how it can be kept fresh for so long without preservatives
How to use this?
It's on
It sterilizes many things
Let's try the phone
Is this right?

Should be?
There are a lot of preservatives in instant foods
And I was told that it's not good to eat a lot of preservatives
So I think it's a huge plus that it doesn't have that
Nothing to worry about
Next,
What's Germinated Brown Rice?
It's hot!
This is half brown rice half regular rice

Brown rice

Black rice

Just regular brown rice

It's delicious

I was wondering why Hetbahn came up with sunscreen

This cosmetic product includes rice bran that was produced from here in South Korea
It preserves the food's taste and smell
They say that the rice bran is the reason why the rice is so tasty
It's actually used in a lot of cosmetic products
It brightens the tone of your face too
Putting on sunscreen in the middle of a mukbang
This is just what I like

I enjoyed their products anyway

Next one

12 bowls

I tried 12 bowls of Hetbahn instant rice today
It's the most I've eaten so far But certainly not my max
And as for that big red box that I showed earlier in the video,
That can be obtained through the campaign website
Watch 3 of their Relief videos

50 people will receive instant rice for a whole year
